message:
feat(dashboard): add WebSocket handler with gorilla/websocket

Adds WebSocketHandler that upgrades HTTP connections to WebSocket,
creates Client instances, and manages read/write goroutines with
proper ping/pong handling for connection keep-alive.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,8 +1,12 @@
 package dashboard
 
 import (
+	"net/http/httptest"
+	"strings"
 	"testing"
 	"time"
+
+	"github.com/gorilla/websocket"
 )
 
 func TestHub_BroadcastMessage(t *testing.T) {
@@ -54,3 +58,36 @@ func TestHub_ClientUnsubscribe(t *testing.T) {
 	// Broadcast should not panic
 	hub.Broadcast("task-1", []byte("test"))
 }
+
+func TestWebSocketHandler_Connect(t *testing.T) {
+	hub := NewHub()
+	go hub.Run()
+	defer hub.Stop()
+
+	handler := NewWebSocketHandler(hub)
+	server := httptest.NewServer(handler)
+	defer server.Close()
+
+	// Convert http:// to ws://
+	wsURL := "ws" + strings.TrimPrefix(server.URL, "http") + "?task=task-1"
+
+	conn, _, err := websocket.DefaultDialer.Dial(wsURL, nil)
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("failed to connect: %v", err)
+	}
+	defer conn.Close()
+
+	// Send a test message through the hub
+	hub.Broadcast("task-1", []byte(`{"type":"test"}`))
+
+	// Read the message
+	conn.SetReadDeadline(time.Now().Add(time.Second))
+	_, msg, err := conn.ReadMessage()
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("failed to read message: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	if !strings.Contains(string(msg), "test") {
+		t.Errorf("expected test message, got %s", string(msg))
+	}
+}
